SecureW2 · Autonomous Security Operations

Entra ID – Adaptive Defense
External Automation

Configure SecureW2's Adaptive Defense framework to integrate with Microsoft Entra ID for real-time, event-driven security responses including dynamic group management and certificate revocation.

Microsoft Entra ID Adaptive Defense ASO – External Actions JoinNow Management Portal

1Introduction

The Adaptive Defense framework is designed to provide dynamic, real-time security responses to evolving threats. By acting as a central intelligence hub that aggregates data across multiple platforms, the JoinNow policy engine can execute granular remediation steps that individual integrations cannot achieve in isolation.

The following document covers the steps to set up JoinNow Autonomous Security Operations with Microsoft Entra ID.

2Prerequisites

The following are the prerequisites for setting up SecureW2 Adaptive Defense with Microsoft Entra ID:

  1. An active subscription to the Azure portal.
  2. An account in the JoinNow Management Portal with Autonomous Security Operations (ASO) – External Actions subscriptions.

3Configuring Azure

SecureW2 Cloud RADIUS has the industry-unique ability to perform directory lookups during authentication. This enables more policy enforcement options and more robust authentication security. This section shows how to implement RADIUS Lookup in Azure and integrate Entra ID with SecureW2.


The following are the high-level tasks required to configure Azure:

  1. Creating an Application in Azure for Account Lookup.
  2. Creating a Client Secret.
  3. Creating a Provider URL and Client ID.
  4. Providing API Permissions.

3.1 Creating an Application in Azure for Account Lookup

To register a new application in Azure, perform the following steps:

  1. Log in to the Microsoft Azure portal.
  2. Type App registrations in the search box and click App registrations.
  3. On the App registrations page, click New registration.
  4. On the Register an application page, enter the name of the application in the Name field.
  5. In the Supported account types section, specify who can use the application by selecting any one of the following options:
    1. Accounts in this organizational directory only (MSFT only – Single tenant).
    2. Accounts in any organizational directory (Any Microsoft Entra ID tenant– Multitenant).
    3. Accounts in any organizational directory (Any Microsoft Entra ID tenant– Multitenant) and personal Microsoft accounts (e.g., Skype, Xbox).
    4. Personal Microsoft accounts only.
  6. Click Register. The following screen is displayed.
  7. Copy the Application (client) ID, Object ID, and Directory (tenant) ID values to your console.

3.2 Creating a Client Secret

To create a client secret, perform the following steps:

  1. In the left pane, go to Manage, then click Certificates & secrets.
  2. Click New client secret.
  3. In the Add a client secret pop-up window, enter a description for the client secret in the Description field.
  4. From the Expires drop-down list, select the client secret's expiration date.
  5. Click Add.
  6. The client’s secret is displayed under the Value column.
NOTE: Ensure you properly save the client secret on your console, as it is non-recoverable.

3.3 Creating a Provider URL and Client ID

To create a provider URL and client ID, perform the following steps:

  1. Navigate to the Overview section.
  2. Copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID values to your console.
  3. Insert it into the following URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{Directory (tenant) ID}. This should look like this:
    https://login.microsoftonline.com/561bc66f-1d86-4244-8bc4-5eb12cba45ac

  1. Save this for later.

3.4 Providing API Permissions

Finally, provide this application permission to access the data in our Entra ID.

  1. In the left pane, go to Manage, then click API permissions.
  2. Click Add a permission.
  3. Click Microsoft Graph.
  4. If the Access Token Grant Flow attribute is Client Credentials, click on Application Permissions.
    1. Add Device.Read.All, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All, and User.Read.All in your API Permissions to configure Signal Source.
    2. Click Grant admin consent for {your organization} to grant consent for the requested permissions.
  5. If the Access Token Grant Flow attribute is Authorization Code, select Delegated Permissions.
    1. Add GroupMember.ReadWrite.All, User.Read.All and Device.Read.All in your API Permissions to configure Device Signal Source.
    2. Click on Grant admin consent for MSFT.
    3. In the Grant admin consent confirmation pop-up window, click Yes.
  6. The configured APIs are granted consent, and the following screen is displayed.

4Configuring JoinNow

After creating an App in Azure, you need to create a Signal Source in SecureW2 to communicate with Azure.

4.1 Creating a Core Platform to Integrate with Microsoft Entra ID

  1. Navigate to Integrations Hub > Core Platforms.
  2. Click Add.
  3. For the Name field, enter the name of the signal source.
  4. For the Description field, enter a suitable description for the signal source.
  5. From the Type drop-down list, select Entra ID.
  6. Click Save.
  7. The page refreshes and displays the Configuration, Attribute Mapping, and Groups tabs.
  8. Click the Configuration tab:
    1. From the Access Token Grant Flow drop-down list, select one of the following options.
      1. Client Credentials - This option eliminates the need to reauthorize tokens in the Azure portal and is the recommended method.
      2. Authorization Code - This option requires reauthorizing the token in the Azure portal every 90 days.
    2. In the Provider URL field, enter the URL you created earlier using the Directory (tenant) ID: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{Directory (tenant) ID}. This should look like this:
      https://login.microsoftonline.com/561bc66f-1d86-4244-8bc4-5eb12cba45ac
    3. In the Client Id field, enter the Application (client) ID that you retrieved from Azure Portal earlier (refer to the 3.1 Creating an Application in Azure for Account Lookup section).
    4. In the Client Secret field, enter the Client secret you generated in the Azure Portal earlier (refer to the 3.2 Creating a Client Secret section).
    5. Under the Lookup Configuration section, from the Device Lookup via drop-down list, select the required device lookup attribute from the options listed below:
      1. Entra ID Device ID - The lookup is performed using Azure ADID.
      2. Entra ID Device Name - The lookup is performed using the device name. For additional search filters, select the required checkboxes:
        1. Is Managed - checks if the device is managed.
        2. Is Compliant - checks if the device is compliant.

    1. Click Update.

4.2 Configuring Groups

  1. Click on the Groups tab.
  2. Click Add.
  3. In the Local Group field, enter a name for the group to identify it locally.
  4. In the Remote Group field, enter a name for the group configured in Entra ID.
  5. Click Create.
  6. Click Update.

4.3 Adaptive Defense Signal Platforms

This section describes the steps to configure the webhook in the Adaptive Defense Platform.

  1. Navigate to Integration Hub > Adaptive Defense Signal Platforms.
  2. Click Add.
  3. In the Basic section, enter the name of the webhook in the Name field.
  4. In the Description field, enter a brief description of the webhook.
  5. From the Provider drop-down list, select Entra ID.
  6. Click Save. The page refreshes, and the Configuration and Events tabs appear.
  7. Click the Configuration tab.
  8. From the Core Provider drop-down list, select the Entra ID security vendor configured in the 4.1 Creating a Core Platform to Integrate with Azure Event Hooks section.
  9. Click the Events tab. Select the required events for which Adaptive Defense signals must be triggered.

  1. Click Update.

4.4 Configuring Adaptive Defense External Platform

When SecureW2 receives an event from the Core Platform, the Adaptive Defense workflow is triggered. This workflow can perform actions such as adding a user or device to a different group configured by the administrator. During the next RADIUS authentication, the user or device is assigned a new role based on the group configuration and associated with a different network policy.

The Core Platform supports the following actions:

  1. Add a user to a group.
  2. Remove the user from a group.
  3. Add a device to a group.
  4. Remove the device from a group.

To create an Autonomous Security Operations for Core Platforms, follow the given steps:

  1. Navigate to Integration Hub > Autonomous Security Operations.
  2. Click the Add.

  3. In the Name field, enter a name for the external connection.
  4. In the Description field, enter the description for the connection.
  5. From the Type drop-down list, under Core Platforms, select Entra ID.

Figure: Adding Core Platforms

  1. Click Save. The page refreshes, and the Configuration tab is displayed.
  2. Click the Configuration tab.
  3. From the Core Provider drop-down list, select the Entra ID signal source. The Core Provider drop-down list displays the signal sources associated with the type of Autonomous Security Operations that was created.

    NOTE: Only manual configuration is supported with Entra ID.
  4. Actions: Select the type of action to perform. You can select one or more options based on your requirements.
    1. Add User to Group
    2. Remove User from Group
    3. Add Device to Group
    4. Remove Device from Group
NOTE: To perform the above actions, configure the GroupMember.ReadWrite.All permissions in your Entra ID.

  1. Click Update.

4.5 Configuring Adaptive Defense Workflow

Adaptive Defense workflow defines the actions to perform when an event is received from an adaptive defense provider. You can configure the workflow to perform tasks such as suspending a certificate, sending an email notification, or taking other appropriate actions based on the event.

This section describes the steps to configure the Adaptive Defense Workflow.

  1. Navigate to Policy Management > Adaptive Defense.
  2. Scroll down to the Adaptive Defense Workflows.
  3. In Adaptive Defense Workflows, click Add Workflow.
  4. In the Basic section, enter the name of the Adaptive Defense workflow in the Name field.
  5. In the Display Description section, enter a brief description for the Adaptive Defense workflow.
  6. Click Save. The Conditions and Settings are displayed.
  7. Click the Conditions tab. Select the conditions for executing the workflow based on the event received from the provider. The workflow executes based on the selected events.
    1. From the Event Trigger Source drop-down list, select the adaptive defense platform that you created in 4.3 Adaptive Defense Signal Platforms. The relevant events are displayed in the Event Type field.
    2. In the Event Type field, select the events based on your business requirements.
  8. Click the Settings tab to configure the type of action to perform.
  9. Click Add Action.
  10. From the Action drop-down list, select the required action to be performed based on the received trigger signal.
  11. From the using drop-down list, select the required attribute type.
  12. From the Platform drop-down list, select the Autonomous Security Operations platform created in 4.4 Configuring Adaptive Defense External Platform section.
  13. From Add to group/Remove from group drop-down list, select the required group from which the user/device must be added or removed.
  14. Click Save.
